letter_workflow.fcra_notice_of_dispute: This is my formal notice of dispute. I dispute the accuracy, completeness, and/or verifiability of the item identified in this letter. Please conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), review all information I provide with this dispute, forward the dispute and all relevant information to the furnisher when this is a bureau dispute, and correct, update, or delete any information that cannot be verified as accurate and complete. Please mark the item as disputed while the investigation is pending and provide the written results of the investigation, including any corrected report or explanation of the verification method used.

cfpb_official_template_sources: CFPB credit reporting company dispute letter template
cfpb_letter_fields: consumer full name and mailing address
plain-language explanation of why the information is wrong
consumer_notice_contents: identify the account or item being disputed
explain the basis for the dispute
include supporting documents or evidence when available
request correction, update, deletion, or verification results
bureau_dispute_rules: consumer reporting agency must conduct a reasonable reinvestigation when accuracy or completeness is disputed
bureau should forward notice of the dispute and relevant information to the furnisher
bureau should provide written reinvestigation results after completion
bureau dispute path is preferred when the next step needs furnisher duties triggered through bureau notice
CFPB template method: clearly identify the disputed item, explain the problem, request correction, and attach copies of proof
furnisher_dispute_rules: direct furnisher dispute should be sent to the furnisher address shown on the report or other proper direct-dispute address
direct dispute should include enough identifying information, the specific disputed information, the basis for dispute, and supporting evidence
furnisher should conduct a reasonable investigation and review relevant information provided with a proper direct dispute
furnisher should report corrections or stop reporting information that cannot be verified as accurate and complete
CFPB template method: dispute directly with the company that gave the information to the bureau when the furnisher's records appear wrong
credit_vivo_controls: customer approval required before mail, bureau dispute, furnisher dispute, CFPB complaint, state complaint, or attorney escalation
store date sent, recipient, delivery method, tracking number, response due date, response received, and next action
attach evidence packet hash or file reference before sending
record whether the item was marked disputed and whether written results were received

bureau_dispute_procedure.cfpb_template_alignment: Use the CFPB credit reporting company dispute template structure: identify the item, explain the dispute, request correction, and attach supporting copies.
round_1_uses: original creditor and debt buyer both showing active balance
obsolete or missing key date item
packet_checklist: ID and proof of address when needed

furnisher_direct_dispute_procedure.cfpb_template_alignment: Use the CFPB furnisher dispute template structure when the source company, collector, or servicer appears to be reporting wrong information.
prerequisites: specific furnisher item and dispute reason stated
proper furnisher/direct-dispute address confirmed when available
requested_verification: chain of title or assignment where applicable
proof reporting is complete and accurate
written investigation result or correction/deletion notice

cfpb_complaint.trigger: no response, verified-as-accurate with weak support, or repeated inaccurate reporting after dispute history is complete
cfpb_complaint.packet: original dispute, proof of delivery, bureau/furnisher responses, current report excerpt, damages or denial evidence if available
cfpb_complaint.cfpb_method: Use CFPB complaint escalation only after the normal dispute record is clear: who was contacted, when, what was disputed, how the company responded, and what remains wrong.

state_attorney_general.trigger: pattern of non-response, abusive collection conduct, or unresolved state-law concern
state_attorney_general.packet: same evidence packet plus consumer timeline and requested resolution

state_regulator.trigger: licensed collector, lender, or credit repair/regulatory issue needs agency review
state_regulator.packet: collector/furnisher identity, license information if known, dispute timeline, and supporting evidence

attorney_review.trigger: strong FCRA/FDCPA pattern, measurable damages, denial letter, identity theft, mixed file, or repeated verification without reasonable investigation
attorney_review.packet: full dispute history, reports before and after disputes, notices, responses, delivery proofs, and damages evidence

recommended_letter_queue.letter_subject: Direct Dispute of Account Reporting - Collection review
recommended_letter_queue.draft_letter_body:
DRAFT - CUSTOMER REVIEW AND APPROVAL REQUIRED
DO NOT SEND UNTIL CUSTOMER AUTHORIZATION IS VERIFIED

[Customer Name]
[Customer Mailing Address]
[City, State ZIP]

[Date]

To: Furnisher / Collector
[Recipient Address]

Re: Collection review

To Whom It May Concern:

I am disputing the accuracy, completeness, or verifiability of the credit reporting item identified below. Please treat this letter as my formal notice of dispute.

Disputed issue: Collection review
Reason for dispute: This collection item should be reviewed for original creditor, balance, ownership, and reporting details.
Review round: Round 2 — Collection Review

This is my formal notice of dispute. I dispute the accuracy, completeness, and/or verifiability of the item identified in this letter. Please conduct a reasonable investigation under the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), review all information I provide with this dispute, forward the dispute and all relevant information to the furnisher when this is a bureau dispute, and correct, update, or delete any information that cannot be verified as accurate and complete. Please mark the item as disputed while the investigation is pending and provide the written results of the investigation, including any corrected report or explanation of the verification method used.

Please provide the basis for your reporting, including records supporting ownership, balance, status, payment history, date of first delinquency, and authority to report this account.

Account Number: 1234567890
Account Type: Collection
Original Creditor: CAPITAL ONE BANK
Balance: $1,234
Status: Collection
Date Opened: 01/10/2021
Date Reported: 04/01/2026
Remarks: Account placed for collection

Please send your written response to the mailing address above.

Sincerely,

[Customer Signature]
[Customer Printed Name]

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Accuracy, completeness, ownership, and verification of collection reporting.
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: The collection must be accurate, complete, and verifiable. If ownership, balance, or reporting authority cannot be supported, correction or deletion may be appropriate.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Missing or unsupported delinquency date for negative reporting.
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: Negative reporting needs a supportable delinquency timeline so the account is not reported longer than allowed.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Charge-off reporting accuracy, dates, balance, sold/transferred status, and obsolescence risk.
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: A charge-off should not report internally inconsistent status, balance, ownership, or delinquency dates.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Accuracy, completeness, ownership, and verification of collection reporting.
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: The collection must be accurate, complete, and verifiable. If ownership, balance, or reporting authority cannot be supported, correction or deletion may be appropriate.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Accuracy, completeness, ownership, and verification of collection reporting.
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: The collection must be accurate, complete, and verifiable. If ownership, balance, or reporting authority cannot be supported, correction or deletion may be appropriate.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Same account reports different balances across bureaus.
fcra_sections: FCRA 623 furnisher duties after bureau notice
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: The same account should have a supportable balance. Differences may be explainable by timing, but unsupported differences should be corrected.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Same account reports different status/payment condition across bureaus.
fcra_sections: FCRA 623 furnisher duties after bureau notice
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: Status and payment condition should be consistent with the furnisher's records and not materially misleading.

metro2_fcra_review.fcra_focus: Same account reports different key dates across bureaus.
fcra_sections: FCRA 623 furnisher duties after bureau notice
metro2_fcra_review.dispute_theory: Key dates drive legal reporting age and consumer harm. Unsupported date differences should be corrected or deleted.

required_core_fields: Amount Past Due if reporting past due
Date Opened or collection acquisition/open date
Date of First Delinquency for negative reporting
Compliance Condition Code / Dispute Indicator when disputed
present_fields: Amount Past Due if reporting past due
Date Opened or collection acquisition/open date
Compliance Condition Code / Dispute Indicator when disputed
missing_or_needs_validation: Date of First Delinquency for negative reporting
warning_flags: Missing DOFD on negative reporting candidate
validation_notes: A collection should identify the original creditor or the basis for ownership/assignment.
Negative collection reporting should have a supportable Date of First Delinquency timeline.
If disputed, the report should show an appropriate dispute notation or compliance condition after notice.
metro2_requirement_review.dispute_use: Use for collection ownership, original creditor, balance, DOFD, status, and dispute-notation challenges.
metro2_requirement_review.production_note: Credit Vivo checklist. Validate against official licensed CDIA Metro 2 CRRG before production/certification.

required_core_fields: Charge-off Amount or High Credit / Original Amount
Date Last Payment / Date Last Activity when available

field_compliance_audit.metro2_concept: Identifies the reporting furnisher/tradeline.
field_compliance_audit.fcra_basis: FCRA 607(b) accuracy; FCRA 611 reinvestigation if disputed.
field_compliance_audit.verification_ask: Verify the exact furnisher/subscriber name reporting this tradeline.
field_compliance_audit.requested_outcome: No automated correction requested unless source records show an inaccuracy.
field_compliance_audit.source_note: Built from Credit Vivo local FCRA/Metro 2 rule files; not a substitute for attorney/CDIA CRRG validation.

eoscar_public_facts.title: What e-OSCAR is
eoscar_public_facts.detail: e-OSCAR is used by consumer reporting agencies and data furnishers to exchange and respond to credit-history disputes. Consumers submit disputes to bureaus and/or furnishers, not directly inside e-OSCAR.
eoscar_public_facts.source: Credit Vivo local e-OSCAR learning guide; public e-OSCAR getting-started concepts

eoscar_public_facts.title: ACDV path
eoscar_public_facts.detail: When a bureau receives a consumer dispute, it may send an Automated Credit Dispute Verification (ACDV) to the furnisher. The furnisher can verify, update, or correct the information.
eoscar_public_facts.source: Credit Vivo local e-OSCAR learning guide

eoscar_public_facts.title: AUD path
eoscar_public_facts.detail: A furnisher can initiate an Automated Universal Dataform (AUD) to request an out-of-cycle correction. AUD is not a consumer direct-submission path.
eoscar_public_facts.source: Credit Vivo local e-OSCAR learning guide

eoscar_public_facts.title: Best packaging principle
eoscar_public_facts.detail: A strong dispute package identifies each account, exact field, reported value, consumer position, support documents, and requested correction or deletion.
eoscar_public_facts.source: Credit Vivo local e-OSCAR learning guide; FTC/CFPB consumer dispute guidance

acdv_packaging_steps: Create one bureau-specific package for each bureau reporting the error so the issue can survive CRA intake and ACDV routing.
Use one account group per section.
Include exact account name and masked account number.
Name the exact disputed field and reported value.
State the consumer position and factual basis.
Attach exhibits and request correct, update, verify, delete, or block as appropriate.
eoscar_packaging_review.package_hint: Identify the collection account, original creditor, balance, ownership/assignment question, and requested verification.
eoscar_packaging_review.evidence_hint: Credit report page, collector letter, original creditor statement, assignment/sale letter if available.
avoid: Do not send generic 'this is inaccurate' wording by itself.
Do not claim direct e-OSCAR access.
Do not send without customer approval and proof review.
Do not misrepresent facts or create false disputes.
